Ice_Log: 0430: sighted ice field on starboard beam and ahead
0600: Following lead through ice field.

8AM: communicated by radio with Nome to learn ice conditions there. Heavy ice fields, closely packed, from 8 to 10 miles off shore. 8AM to Noon: In ice field. Received radiogram from Nome stating that there was clear water + haze miles off shore. Omitted musters and drill on account of piloting vessel in ice field.
Merid to 1345: Steered general course NNW 1/2 W through leads in ice.
1345: Lead close, moored ship to ice floe.
1400 to 1600: Awaited opening of new leads in ice. 1400: The Corwin moored to ice floe nearby.
1705: unmoored ship from ice and went ahead to the Westward and Northward, various speeds, through heavy field ice.
8PM to Mid: Going various courses and speds through ice until 22:40, when made fast to ice pack  2.5 miles off Nome. Dog teams and sleds started taking mail to Nome.

Note: 2240: Stopped in ice 2.5 miles off Nome

Ice_Log: 2320: SS Corwin made fast to ice pack astern of Bear, passengers anc cargo being transported over the ice pack to Nome by dog teams & sleds.
Mid to 4AM: Vessel moored to ice pack off Nome, Alaska
4AM to 8AM: Conditions good for landing mail by dog sleds over ice.
8AM to Noon: Took 1700 gallons fresh water from pool on ice pack. Ice breaking up in shore forming a lead and making it possible for dog teams to come to ship.
1300: underway, stood around ice pack
Note: 1300: Started 1335: Stopped. Moved to clear water.

Ice_Log: 1945: stood various courses full speed through ice field.
2100: out of ice
2230: skirting ice pack looking for lead. 2240: Unable to locate any lead, turned and skirted ice pack looking for lead to Southwest and Eastward.
Mid to 4AM: Stood various courses and speeds in ice floe, making good general course of ESE. At end worked to bells in ice.
0340: Ahead through leads in ice.
0445: Unable to proceed on account of heavy ice - stopped.
0554: Ahead various speeds, turned and headed for open water again. Increased speed to get free of heavy packed ice.
0848: Moored ship to large floe in open water 8AM to Noon: Omitted quarters and drills on account of vessel being in ice pack.
Note: 8 AM: Cape Nome NW 1/4 N, 9 miles 0848: Stopped

Ice_Log: Noon to 4PM: Vessel moored to ice floe, awaiting opening of leads
Note: Noon: Cape Nome NW 3/8 N, 9 miles

8PM to Mid: Moored to ground ice throughout; no leads opening to Eastward.
0110: ice closed in, unmoored and stood various courses and speeds in southerly direction through leads in ice.
4AM to 6AM: Working various courses and speeds through heavy ice. At 06:00 unable to proceed further to Eastward on account of heavy ice, turned and stood out of pack.
0715: clear of heavy ice, stood to Westward and Southward along edge of ice pack.

8AM to Merid: Stood on various courses around and through various leads in ice field, making good general course toward St. Michael. Omitted quarters and drills on account of piloting vessel through ice.
